problem.instruction: Read the passage in <A> and the examples in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
Many inflectional affixes in English are sensitive to phonological properties of base words that they attach to. One example is the comparative affix -er that attaches to adjectives as follows:

(1) Base adjectives → Suffixed comparatives
smart → smarter
intelligent → *intelligenter
pretty → prettier
attractive → *attractiver

It appears that the attested base adjectives in (1) are no longer than two syllables. Consider the following words in (2), in which the comparative affix -er attaches to disyllabic adjectives.

(2) Base adjectives → Suffixed comparatives
happy → happier
tiny → tinier
brainy → brainier
mighty → mightier

While the affix -er attaches to various adjectives, the words in (3) indicate that not all disyllabic adjectives can take the affix -er.

(3) Base adjectives → Suffixed comparatives
afraid → *afraider
naked → *nakeder
active → *activer
verdant → *verdanter

Note: '*' indicates unattested comparatives.

<B>
a. avid   b. fancy   c. jealous   d. narrow

Based on <A>, choose the TWO adjectives in <B> which the comparative affix -er can attach to. Then, based on <A> and <B>, state the phonological conditions for the attested and unattested disyllabic adjectives, respectively.

blanks.label: ②
blanks.context: phonological condition for attested disyllabic adjectives

blanks.label: ③
blanks.context: phonological condition for unattested disyllabic adjectives

steps.label: attested 패턴 분석 — (2)번
steps.explanation: happy, tiny, brainy, mighty는 모두 -er를 취한다. 각 단어의 마지막 음절을 확인하면: hap-py /pi/, ti-ny /ni/, brain-y /ni/, might-y /ti/ — 모두 모음으로 끝나는 open syllable이다.

steps.label: unattested 패턴 분석 — (3)번
steps.explanation: afraid /freɪd/, naked /kɪd/, active /tɪv/, verdant /dənt/ — 각 단어의 마지막 음절이 자음으로 끝나는 closed syllable임을 확인할 수 있다. coda가 있는 syllable에는 -er가 붙지 않는다.

steps.label: <B> 분류 적용
steps.explanation: a. avid: /æv.ɪd/ → 마지막 음절 /ɪd/ — coda /d/ 있음 → 불가. b. fancy: /fæn.si/ → 마지막 음절 /si/ — 모음 /i/로 끝남 → 가능 ✓. c. jealous: /dʒel.əs/ → 마지막 음절 /əs/ — coda /s/ 있음 → 불가. d. narrow: /næ.roʊ/ → 마지막 음절 /roʊ/ — 이중모음 /oʊ/로 끝남 → 가능 ✓. 가능 이름: b(fancy), d(narrow).

steps.label: 음운 조건 정리 — attested vs. unattested
steps.explanation:
attested 조건: 2음절 형용사의 마지막 음절이 open syllable — rhyme이 nucleus만으로 이루어진 경우(모음으로 끝남, coda 없음).
unattested 조건: 마지막 음절이 closed syllable — rhyme이 nucleus + coda 자음을 포함한 경우(자음으로 끝남).
이 조건은 음절 유형(open syllable / closed syllable)이지, 단순 모음/자음 개수가 아님.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — 음절 구조 분석 원칙
steps.explanation:
함정 주의:
① 마지막 글자가 자음이면 closed, 모음이면 open이라고 단순화하기 쉽지만, 반드시 음운 전사([])로 확인해야 한다. 'narrow' /næ.roʊ/는 철자상 'w'로 끝나지만 발음은 /roʊ/로 이중모음 → open syllable.
② 마지막 음절의 coda 유무가 결정적 기준. rhyme = nucleus(+coda) 구조를 파악할 것.
③ 'open syllable' / 'closed syllable' 용어를 정확히 사용하여 답안 서술.

key_terms.definition_en: A syllable whose rhyme consists of only a nucleus, with no coda consonant.
key_terms.definition_ko: 음절의 rhyme이 핵모음만으로 이루어진 음절. coda 자음 없음.
key_terms.exam_usage: 비교급 -er의 결합 조건으로 출제 — 마지막 음절이 open syllable이면 -er 결합 가능

key_terms.definition_en: A syllable whose rhyme consists of a nucleus plus one or more coda consonants.
key_terms.definition_ko: 음절의 rhyme이 핵모음 + coda 자음으로 이루어진 음절.
key_terms.exam_usage: 마지막 음절이 closed syllable이면 -er 결합 불가

model_answer.essay_content: For the attested disyllabic adjectives, the last syllable is an open syllable whose rhyme consists of only a nucleus (ends in a vowel). For the unattested disyllabic adjectives, the last syllable is a closed syllable whose rhyme consists of a nucleus and a coda (ends in a consonant).

scoring_criteria.criterion: ① fancy, narrow 두 개 모두 선택
attested 조건: 마지막 음절이 open syllable (모음으로 끝남, coda 없음)
unattested 조건: 마지막 음절이 closed syllable (자음으로 끝남, coda 있음)

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: The attested disyllabic adjectives end in a vowel. The unattested disyllabic adjectives end in a consonant.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: When the last syllable of a disyllabic adjective is an open syllable with a rhyme composed of only a nucleus, the comparative suffix -er can be attached. When the last syllable is a closed syllable with a rhyme composed of a nucleus and a coda, the suffix -er cannot be attached.

references.author: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams
references.title: An Introduction to Language (11th ed.)
references.excerpt: An open syllable ends in a vowel (nucleus only); a closed syllable ends in a consonant (nucleus + coda). Morphological processes in English are often sensitive to syllable structure: the comparative suffix -er attaches freely to open-final disyllabic adjectives but is blocked from bases whose final syllable is closed.

references.author: Plag, I.
references.title: Word-Formation in English
references.excerpt: The distribution of comparative -er is morphophonologically constrained. Disyllabic adjectives with final open syllables (ending in a vowel, such as -y, -ow) readily accept -er, while those with final closed syllables (ending in a coda consonant, such as -ent, -ive, -id) are systematically blocked. This illustrates how syllable structure — the presence or absence of a coda — can condition the attachment of an inflectional affix.

source.concept_explanation: The comparative suffix -er in English is morphophonologically conditioned: it attaches to disyllabic adjectives only when the final syllable is an open syllable (rhyme = nucleus only, ending in a vowel). Disyllabic adjectives with a closed final syllable (rhyme = nucleus + coda, ending in a consonant) are unattested with -er. This is an example of an inflectional affix sensitive to the phonological structure of its base — specifically, syllable structure (open vs. closed syllable) rather than simple vowel/consonant count.
